education.notes: A.B. Yale 1937. Attended Asheville School prior to Yale. Chaired class capital gifts committee 15 years; won Yale Medal for Distinguished Service; named Sterling Fellow.

career.notes: Retired from Pickands Mather to found investment management firm.

institutions.institution_blurb: Yale A.B. 1937; Yale Medal for Distinguished Service; Sterling Fellow.

narrative_blocks.header: Chaired Yale class gifts; won Distinguished Service Medal
narrative_blocks.body: For fifteen years Arms chaired his Yale class capital gifts committee — work that earned him the Yale Medal for Distinguished Service and the title of Sterling Fellow of the University. He graduated Yale in the class of 1937 and spent thirty years at Pickands Mather's Cleveland steel operations before founding Financial Management and Resources Company.

narrative_blocks.header: Married 73 years; the family called him 'Poppy'
narrative_blocks.body: Arms married Betsy Parcells in July 1939 and the marriage lasted until his death at ninety-seven, seventy-three years later — a span longer than most genealogical generations. His family knew him as 'Poppy,' a Pied Piper who shared his passion for sailing, fishing, and lobster pots with children and grandchildren alike.

education.notes: B.A. Art 1939. Served as Class President and Head of Student Government.

institutions.institution_blurb: Connecticut College B.A. Art 1939; Class President and Head of Student Government.

narrative_blocks.header: Class president; wed same year she graduated
narrative_blocks.body: Elizabeth 'Betsy' Parcells graduated Connecticut College in 1939 as both Class President and Head of Student Government, then married Charles Snelling Arms on July 3rd of the same year — a summer wedding that ended her Connecticut years and began thirty in Cleveland, Ohio. Her marriage to Arms lasted nearly seventy-three years, until his death in 2012 at ninety-seven.

narrative_blocks.header: Founded Ocean Reef Chapel in Florida semi-retirement
narrative_blocks.body: In semi-retirement at Ocean Reef, Key Largo, she helped found both the Ocean Reef Chapel and the Ocean Reef Art League, channeling a lifelong passion for watercolor and community building. She and Charles divided their later years between Key Largo winters and Marion, Massachusetts summers.

education.notes: Graduated summa cum laude 1991 with double major in psychology and economics.
MBA 1995 with certificate in Non-Profit Management.

career.organization: Dresner RCM Capital Management, San Francisco
American Research and Management Company, Marion MA
career.notes: His father had helped found the firm.

narrative_blocks.header: Stanford MBA, McKinsey consultant; died at thirty-nine
narrative_blocks.body: Jonathan Arms graduated Dartmouth summa cum laude in 1991 and earned a Stanford MBA in 1995, then built a career as a McKinsey consultant before returning to Marion, Massachusetts to manage investments at a firm his father had helped found. He died unexpectedly at his Providence, Rhode Island home in October 2008, aged thirty-nine, survived by two young children.

burialCemetery.name: Evergreen Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Marion Plymouth County MA. Burial site of Elizabeth 'Betsy' Parcells Arms (HD4334) and Charles Snelling Arms (I00698).

INST022.name: American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions
INST022.notes: First American foreign-mission organization. Tree connections: Rev. Elias Cornelius (agent 1816, secretary 1831-32); Ainsworth and Harriet Ellsworth Blunt, missionaries to the Cherokee at Brainerd, Tennessee; Eliza Talcott, missionary to Japan; Rev. William Banfield Capron, Madura mission; Judge John Hooker, member.
